publicationstmt.publisher: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

profiledesc.creation: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2020-01-14 23:48:13 -0500.

repository.corpname: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

did.abstract: Collection documents aspects of Greek arts, music, rebetika, literature, and politics. Publications, photographs, interviews, and correspondence relating to Mikis Theodorakis, Nikos Kavadias, and Iakōvos Kampanellēs, amongst others. Also recordings of Greek music. Also poetry related items including correspondence with Jon Stallworthy. Angelakē-Rouk, Katerina. Correspondence between Miss Ella Dunkel and Netty Palmer. Articles and other materials related to the Greek Junta and Holst-Warhaft's work with the anti-junta organizations in Australia and elsewhere.

scopecontent.head: Content Description
scopecontent.p: Collection documents aspects of Greek arts, music, rebetika, literature, and politics. Publications, photographs, interviews, and correspondence relating to Mikis Theodorakis, Nikos Kavadias, and Iakōvos Kampanellēs, amongst others. Also recordings of Greek music. Also poetry related items including correspondence with Jon Stallworthy. Angelakē-Rouk, Katerina. Correspondence between Miss Ella Dunkel and Netty Palmer. Articles and other materials related to the Greek Junta and Holst-Warhaft's work with the anti-junta organizations in Australia and elsewhere.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Gail Holst-Warhaft served as an adjunct Professor in Classics, Comparative Literature and Near Eastern Studies at Cornell University. She also served as the Director of the Mediterranean Studies Initiative and Faculty Associate of the Cornell Center for a Sustainable Future. Areas of focus include Modern Greek Literature and Music and Greek Literature from Antiquity to the Present. She is also a poet, translator, and musician.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Gail Holst-Warhaft papers, #14-9-4394. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.

did.unittitle: Series VII. Correspondence with John Stallworthy, Vance Palmer, Nettie Palmer, and Ella Dunkel
Music by Gustav Holst and M. Holst; correspondence with Imogen Holst
Nettie Palmer, Ella Dunkel, and Jon Stallworthy correspondence & poems

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Nettie Palmer is a well-known Australian communist writer, Ella Dunkel is a family friend, and Jon Stallworthy is a British poet and literary biographer.

did.unittitle: Jon Stallworthy poems and correspondence - Folder I

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes drafts of poems and prose work by both Gail Holst-Warhaft and Jon Stallworthy, with commentary.

did.unittitle: Jon Stallworthy poems and correspondence - Folder II

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes drafts of poems and prose work by both Gail Holst-Warhaft and Jon Stallworthy, with commentary.

did.unittitle: Jon Stallworthy poems and correspondence - Folder III

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes drafts of poems and prose work by both Gail Holst-Warhaft and Jon Stallworthy, with commentary.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Iakovos Kambanellis is one of Greece's leading playwrights. Gail Holst-Warhaft translated Kambanellis' memoir, Mauthausen, concerning his imprisonment in Mauthausen concentration camp.

did.unittitle: Correspondence with Alison Gold regarding Iakovos Kambanellis documentary

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Items in Greek, English, and German.

scopecontent.p: Some programs very rare.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes articles, reviews, and correspondence. Published by Hakkert, Amsterdam in 1980. Humanities Press. English Edition.
